raw_consumer_statement:
This complaint relates to my previous CFPB case XXXX XXXX. XXXX regarding a {$26000.00} charge from XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>, which appeared on my XX/XX/year> billing statement. 

I was coerced into authorizing this charge and did not willingly approve it. I first disputed the charge with American Express on XX/XX/year>. The dispute was closed without crediting my account. I provided additional supporting documentation on XX/XX/year>, but XXXX XXXX decision remained unchanged. 

Since then, I have obtained new documentation that further supports my claim : XXXX XXXX  letter confirming the same transaction was treated as unauthorized and that they closed my account accordingly. 

An additional paper I previously had, which also supports that I did not willingly authorize this charge. 

American Express letter dated XX/XX/year>, denying my dispute but acknowledging the investigation. 

Another financial institution independently reviewed this same transaction and concluded it was not authorized, reinforcing my dispute and showing that Amexs denial did not fully consider all evidence. 

The repeated denials, lack of resolution, and inadequate consideration of my documentation have caused significant stress and emotional burden. I request that the CFPB require American Express to reopen the investigation, review this charge as disputed due to coercion/unauthorized circumstances, and credit or remove the {$26000.00} from my account. Please reference my previous complaint ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) for all prior documentation, including screenshots, communications, and supporting evidence.
